⚡ Key Takeaways — NUBANK
NU has 1,309 institutional holders vs BAP's 694. Larger but less compatible base — NU compat avg (4.6/8) well below BAP's (6.8/8). 187 firms hold both.
US dominates NU (61%), with Brazil representation (38 firms) reflecting NU's home market. Hedge fund share (18%) elevated vs BAP's 8.2%.
187 firms hold both NU and BAP — fastest conversion pool, pre-educated on LatAm digital-financials thesis.
NU avg saturation (52.7%) vs BAP's (24.6%) — BAP holders have 4× more headroom. Key message for BAP deepening strategy.
Aware Super, Boston Partners, and Amundi Ireland are top BAP priority targets — high compatibility, low saturation, no BAP exposure.
⚡ Key Takeaways — CREDICORP (BAP)
BAP has a well-diversified base — 694 firms across 30+ countries. Top 5 holders represent ~15% of shares.
Dodge & Cox (4.88% OS) and MFS (3.39%) near fully weighted positions — not expansion targets. Focus on deepening conviction.
BAP has 182 NU-crossover investors not yet in BAP — highest-quality pipeline, pre-qualified for Credicorp IR outreach.
BAP avg compatibility (6.8/8) far exceeds NU's (4.6/8) — premium quality, mandate-matched institutional base.
US concentration (51% of BAP shareholders) creates geographic risk. Asia and Europe Continental remain key underdeveloped opportunities.

🎯 Credicorp (BAP) IR Targeting Strategy
Prioritized investor outreach plan for BAP — who to target, in which regions, with what urgency
📐 Targeting Score Methodology — BAP-Focused
Score (0–100) quantifies attractiveness for Credicorp IR outreach. Uses log-normalized upside for dispersion.

① BAP Upside (40%) — Gap to fully weighted position. ② Room to Grow (30%) — Inverse saturation. ③ Compatibility (20%) — 1–8 scale. ④ New Investor Bonus (10%) — Not yet in BAP.

P1 (top 33% ≥ 74.3): Road show immediately  |  P2 (mid 33% 65.3–74.3): Outbound outreach  |  P3 (bot 33%): Monitor

🚨 Key Insights & Strategic Recommendations
Data-driven Credicorp IR strategy — where to focus, who to target, how to prioritize
A. KEY INSIGHTS
NU has 1,309 institutional holders vs BAP's 694 — larger but less compatible. NU compat avg (4.6/8) well below BAP's (6.8/8).
BAP saturation is dramatically lower than NU's (24.6% vs 52.7%) — BAP investors have 4× more headroom. Key deepening message.
187 firms hold both NU and BAP. Pre-educated on LatAm digital-financials — fastest conversion path. Require differentiated messaging, not education.
BAP underpenetrated in Asia: 19 BAP holders vs 83+ NU holders. Asian sovereign capital holds NU but not BAP — warm introduction via NU community.
NU Hedge Fund overrepresentation (18%) signals tactical holders. BAP's balanced Growth/Value mix provides more stable long-term base quality.
182 BAP priority targets: NU-crossover investors not yet in BAP — pre-qualified pipeline for Credicorp IR outreach.
B. Regional Targeting Strategy — Credicorp (BAP)
🇺🇸
United States — Deepen & Convert
US has 138 BAP holders. Unconverted NU-US holders (Growth/GARP mandates) are the main pipeline. Focus: mid-tier growth funds with EM sleeves not yet in BAP.
→ Morgan Stanley & Goldman EM conferences · Q2 2026
🌏
Asia — Critical Gap, Highest ROI
83 NU holders in Asia vs 19 BAP holders. NPS Korea, Nomura AM, Sumitomo Mitsui — hold NU but not BAP. Single Asia NDR converts 3–5 high-priority institutions.
→ Priority 1: Singapore / Tokyo NDR · H1 2026
🌍
Europe Continental — Underpenetrated
37 BAP holders vs 174 NU holders in Continental Europe. NBIM, AMF Pension, Allianz GI — all in NU universe but absent from BAP.
→ Frankfurt / Zurich / Stockholm circuit · H2 2026
🇬🇧
United Kingdom — Deepen Existing
46 NU holders vs 59 BAP holders in UK — well-penetrated for BAP. Focus: deepening existing BAP positions rather than NU-to-BAP conversion.
→ London NDRs · Quarterly cadence
🌎
Brazil — Niche but Strategic
10 NU holders in Brazil vs 3 BAP holders. Emphasize Peru/Andean diversification vs NU's Brazil concentration for Brazil-based LatAm mandate funds.
→ Targeted outreach · Opportunistic
C. Top 15 Priority BAP Targets
NU crossover → BAP · Click for profile
D. STRATEGIC NARRATIVE
BAP saturation (24.6%) is the headline message. Current BAP holders can absorb 4× more capital. Priority: building conviction, deepening positions.
The 187 crossover firms are the fastest lever. BAP vs NU pitch: same LatAm ecosystem, different risk profile (Peru stability vs Brazil volatility), complementary returns.
Asia is the single biggest structural opportunity. NU has 83 Asian holders vs BAP's 19. Japanese/Korean sovereign capital holds NU — warm BAP introduction available.
NU's high saturation (52.7%) means NU-focused investors are at capacity. The pitch: diversify LatAm fintech exposure with BAP's different risk/return profile.
📚 Glossary & Methodology
Reference guide for all variables, scores, and interpretation frameworks
A. Variable Definitions
Upside
Additional shares an investor could buy before reaching Fully Weighted Position.
Upside = Fully Weighted Position − Current Position
Fundamental Compatibility (1–8 scale)
Data provider score measuring mandate alignment with the stock's characteristics.
1–3 = Poor · 4–5 = Moderate · 6–7 = Strong · 8 = Exceptional
Fully Weighted Position
Estimated shares investor would hold if stock were at natural model portfolio weight. The demand ceiling.
Saturation
% of Fully Weighted Position currently held. Low = room to grow.
Saturation (%) = (Current Position ÷ Fully Weighted Position) × 100

<30% = Underweight (opportunity) · 30–70% = Moderate · >70% = Near capacity
Portfolio %
Stock's current weight as % of investor's total AUM. Higher = higher conviction.
Peers in Portfolio
Number of comparable peers investor holds (ITUB, BCI, CIB, BBD, GFNORTE). More peers = broader EM mandate = natural BAP buyer.
B. Targeting Score — Construction
What It Measures
Composite metric (0–100) quantifying attractiveness for Credicorp IR outreach. Uses log-normalized upside for proper dispersion across the full investor range.
Score Components
① BAP Upside (log-normalized)
40%
② Room to Grow (100% − saturation)
30%
③ Fundamental Compatibility (1–8)
20%
④ New Investor Bonus (not in BAP)
10%
Score Interpretation
ScoreTierAction
≥ 74.3P1Allocate road show slot immediately
65.3–74.3P2Outbound email/call outreach
< 65.3P3Monitor only — no active IR spend

Geographic Regions
Standard taxonomy: US · North America (ex-US) · UK · Europe Continental · Asia · LatAm (ex-Brazil) · Brazil · Middle East · Africa.
